description: This pipeline appears to be designed for processing and analyzing biomedical images, specifically focusing on tasks such as image calibration, filtering, conversion between different formats, and overlaying multiple images. It begins by taking input from various sources, including compartments, and then applies a series of operations to these inputs, including copying physical dimensions, converting labels to regions of interest (ROIs), and changing ROI properties. The pipeline also involves exporting images into non-native formats and blending different images together. Overall, the goal seems to be to prepare and transform image data for further analysis or visualization, potentially involving cell membrane and mask analysis, distance calculations between different parts of the images, and organizing data based on annotations.

bd8cf641-b46f-4756-8a53-c72ecf83d5d2.name: iji-export-image-v2
bd8cf641-b46f-4756-8a53-c72ecf83d5d2.description:
Exports incoming images into a non-JIPipe format (PNG, JPEG, BMP, AVI, TIFF). Please note support for input images depending on the file format:
  • PNG, JPEG, BMP: 2D images only
  • AVI: 2D or 3D images only
  • TIFF: All images supported


This algorithm groups the incoming data based on the annotations. Those groups can consist of one data item per slot.

cf4755cd-fd2e-467f-83c6-c3f6e22db44c.name: ij3d-filter-roi3d-by-statistics
cf4755cd-fd2e-467f-83c6-c3f6e22db44c.description:
Filters the 3D ROI list elements via statistics.

This algorithm groups the incoming data based on the annotations. Those groups can consist of one data item per slot.

8398880e-fa5b-4473-8bba-6006df02eacf.name: ij3d-roi-convert-to-rgb
8398880e-fa5b-4473-8bba-6006df02eacf.description:
Converts 3D ROI lists to a label image. Depending on the number of objects, an 8-bit, 16-bit, or 32-bit label image is generated.

This algorithm groups the incoming data based on the annotations. Those groups can consist of one data item per slot.
